filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: First differentiation within consciousness where self-awareness emerges while maintaining unity
filteredProps.function: First subtle manifestation (abhāsa) where "I" principle (aham) appears. Śiva and Śakti in equilibrium. Emphasis on Icchā (divine will). Experience "I am this (universe)". Represents Absolute Sovereignty.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Divine will (iccha) predominant, with "I am this universe" experience where "I" remains primary
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Establishes the primordial subject-object relationship with I-consciousness dominant, enabling divine will
filteredProps.description: Sadashiva represents the first subtle manifestation where the divine I-principle (aham) appears. The experience is "I am this universe" with emphasis on the "I".
filteredProps.name: Sadashiva Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The first stirring of self-awareness and will within the divine, beginning of subject-object polarity
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Sadashiva Tattva - The Eternal I-Am Consciousness
filteredProps.coreNature: The equilibrium of subject and object where divine I-consciousness (aham) first emerges with universal This-ness (idam) subordinate
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Realizing the eternal I-am nature of consciousness that encompasses all experience

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness experiencing itself as the totality of manifestation, the cosmic Lord
filteredProps.function: Phase where Jñāna (divine knowledge) is prominent. Experience "This (universe) is myself". Corresponds to divine concealment (tirodhāna). Seeds of separation appear as God and creation.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Divine knowledge (jnana) predominant, with "This universe is myself" experience where "This" is primary
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Establishes divine lordship through knowledge power, preparing for the concealment that enables individual souls
filteredProps.description: Ishvara Tattva represents divine lordship where knowledge (jnana) is prominent. The experience shifts to "This universe is myself" with emphasis on "This".
filteredProps.name: Ishvara Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The emergence of object-awareness within the divine, preparation for cosmic manifestation
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Ishvara Tattva - Divine Lordship and Omniscient Knowledge
filteredProps.coreNature: The reversal where This-consciousness (idam) becomes prominent while I-consciousness (aham) recedes, enabling divine knowledge
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Realizing divine lordship within consciousness, seeing all as one's own manifestation

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness in perfect subject-object balance, ready for diverse manifestation
filteredProps.function: Pure knowledge (Sadvidyā). Balanced "I" and "this". Divine intellect making true knowledge possible. Creative activity and wisdom balanced. Tri-mūrti emerge. Allows identification with One and many.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Divine action (kriya) predominant, with balanced "I" and "This" enabling pure creative activity
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Bridge between pure and pure-impure tattvas, enabling the trimurti functions while maintaining divine unity
filteredProps.description: Shuddha Vidya represents pure knowledge where I-consciousness and This-consciousness are perfectly balanced, bridging pure and pure-impure realms.
filteredProps.name: Shuddha Vidya Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The perfect balance preparing for limitation, emergence of diversity within unity
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Shuddha Vidya Tattva - Pure Knowledge and Balanced Awareness
filteredProps.coreNature: Perfect equilibrium of I-consciousness and This-consciousness, where subject and object are equally balanced in pure knowledge
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Realizing balanced awareness that knows both self and other as one reality

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Transitional consciousness experiencing progressive limitation from universal to individual
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Pure-Impure Tattvas - The Seven Limiting Instruments of Individuation
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Domain of cosmic limitation where the infinite becomes finite through systematic contraction
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Creates the interface between absolute and empirical reality through progressive veiling via maya and the five kanchukas
filteredProps.description: The Pure-Impure Tattvas constitute the limiting instruments through which infinite consciousness contracts itself to appear as finite individual souls through maya and the five kanchukas.
filteredProps.coreNature: Intermediate reality levels where universal consciousness contracts into individual experience through maya and self-imposed limitations
filteredProps.name: Shuddhashuddha Tattvas
filteredProps.internalStructure: Seven tattvas in mod8 structure: Maya (#2-2-1-0/1), Kala-limitation (#2-2-1-2), Vidya (#2-2-1-3), Raga (#2-2-1-4), Kala-time (#2-2-1-5), Niyati (#2-2-1-6), Purusha (#2-2-1-7). Maya as 0/1 represents the non-dual veiling principle linking pure to impure.

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness beginning to experience itself as limited and separate
filteredProps.function: Cosmic illusion, veiling force. Makes the universal Self appear as many. Introduces fragmentation and difference.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Power of differentiation and veiling that makes the One appear as many
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Primary limiting principle that enables individual experience by concealing the universal nature of consciousness
filteredProps.description: Maya Tattva is the cosmic illusion that makes the universal Self appear as many. As 0/1, it represents both void of separation and first principle of limitation.
filteredProps.name: Maya Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The principle that begins separation of individual consciousness from universal, enabling finite experience
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Maya Tattva - The Cosmic Veiling Power and Principle of Differentiation
filteredProps.coreNature: The divine power that veils unity and creates the appearance of separation, multiplicity, and limitation
filteredProps.limitationType: Creates the fundamental illusion of separateness
filteredProps.kanchukaFunction: Not a kanchuka itself but the source of all five kanchukas
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending the illusion of separation to recognize the unity behind apparent multiplicity

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness experiencing itself as having limited power and ability
filteredProps.function: Limitation in Power (Kañcuka). Restricts omnipotent Śakti to limited doership (individual agency).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Limitation of divine omnipotence into restricted agency and partial efficacy
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Constrains the infinite creative power of consciousness into limited individual capacity
filteredProps.description: Kala as limitation restricts infinite powers. It limits omnipotence into finite agency, creating the experience of "I can do only this much."
filteredProps.name: Kala Tattva (Limitation)
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The contraction of infinite potential into limited expression and bounded capability
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Kala Tattva - The Limitation of Infinite Power into Finite Agency
filteredProps.coreNature: First kanchuka that restricts omnipotence into limited ability to act and accomplish
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending limitations to recognize the infinite power of consciousness within

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness experiencing itself as incomplete and seeking satisfaction externally
filteredProps.function: Limitation in Fullness (Kañcuka). Introduces desire/attachment. Obscures infinite bliss, leading to lack and longing.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Limitation of divine fullness into desire, creating attachment and aversion
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Constrains the self-complete nature of consciousness into seeking fulfillment through objects
filteredProps.description: Raga creates desire by limiting infinite fullness (purnatva). The complete Self appears to lack, generating attraction and aversion.
filteredProps.name: Raga Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The emergence of desire and attachment from the veiling of natural completeness
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Raga Tattva - The Limitation of Fullness into Desire and Attachment
filteredProps.coreNature: Third kanchuka that restricts infinite completeness into experiences of lack, desire, and attachment
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending desire to recognize the inherent fullness of consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness experiencing itself as bound by time and temporal sequence
filteredProps.function: Limitation in Time (Kañcuka). Causes timeless Self to experience sequential time and duration.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Limitation of divine eternity into temporal existence and sequential experience
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Constrains the timeless nature of consciousness into past-present-future temporality
filteredProps.description: Kala as time limits eternal (nitya) nature into temporal sequence, creating experiences of change, aging, and mortality.
filteredProps.name: Kala Tattva (Time)
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The limitation of eternal now into sequential time flow and temporal existence
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Kala Tattva - The Limitation of Eternity into Temporal Sequence
filteredProps.coreNature: Fourth kanchuka that restricts eternal presence into sequential time experience
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending temporal limitations to recognize the eternal nature of consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness experiencing itself as bound by space, causality, and natural law
filteredProps.function: Limitation in Space/Order (Kañcuka). Causes all-pervading consciousness to experience spatial order and causality (fixed destiny).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Limitation of divine freedom into fixed order, destiny, and cause-effect relationships
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Constrains the all-pervasive free nature of consciousness into spatial and causal boundaries
filteredProps.description: Niyati creates spatial limitation and causal order, completing the five veils that create individual limitation.
filteredProps.name: Niyati Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The binding of infinite freedom into spatial order and causal necessity
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Niyati Tattva - The Limitation of Freedom into Causality and Order
filteredProps.coreNature: Fifth kanchuka that restricts omnipresence and freedom into spatial limitation and causal determinism
filteredProps.limitationType: Limits omnipresence to spatial order and causality
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending spatial-causal limitations to recognize omnipresent freedom

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness fully identified as a separate individual being
filteredProps.function: Individual Self or limited experiencer. Soul bound by ignorance, perceiving itself as separate.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Limited experiencer characterized by all five limitations, ready to engage with prakriti
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Represents the fully individuated consciousness that will experience the material world
filteredProps.description: Purusha is the individual self bound by ignorance. Liberation involves recognizing this limited purusha is actually Shiva.
filteredProps.name: Purusha Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The fully formed individual consciousness experiencing itself as separate from the whole
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Purusha Tattva - The Limited Individual Soul or Experiencer
filteredProps.coreNature: The individual self or soul (anu) bound by maya and the five kanchukas, experiencing itself as separate
filteredProps.individualAspect: The atomic self (anu) or individual soul in bondage
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending separate selfhood to recognize identity with universal consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness veiled by matter, operating through psychophysical instruments
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Impure Tattvas - The Twenty-Four Material and Psychophysical Principles
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Domain of prakriti where consciousness appears as insentient matter and limited mental faculties
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Manifests the complete domain of mind-matter experience for the limited individual through Samkhya categories
filteredProps.description: The Impure Tattvas encompass the entire domain of matter and mind as experienced by the limited Purusha through prakriti and its evolutes.
filteredProps.coreNature: Material and psychophysical reality levels characterized by duality, unconsciousness, and the play of three gunas
filteredProps.name: Ashuddha Tattvas
filteredProps.internalStructure: Twenty-four tattvas organized in five subcategories: Prakriti root (#2-2-2-0), Antahkarana (#2-2-2-1), Jnanendriyas (#2-2-2-2), Karmendriyas (#2-2-2-3), Tanmatras (#2-2-2-4), Mahabhutas (#2-2-2-5)

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: The unconscious material principle that serves as the object for consciousness
filteredProps.function: Primordial Nature, subtle matrix of matter. Root of insentient existence. Contains three guṇas (sattva, rajas, tamas) in equilibrium.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Unconscious primal matter that evolves into the manifest world when activated by purusha's presence
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the substantial basis for all psychophysical manifestation experienced by purusha
filteredProps.description: Prakriti is primordial matter containing three gunas in equilibrium, the root of all insentient existence.
filteredProps.name: Prakriti Tattva
filteredProps.gunaComposition: Sattva, Rajas, and Tamas in perfect equilibrium
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The foundation of material existence from which all forms emerge
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Prakriti Tattva - Primordial Matter and Root Substance
filteredProps.coreNature: The unmanifest primordial matter containing three gunas in equilibrium, root of all material manifestation
resonances: Binah as cosmic mother in Kabbalah
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with matter to recognize consciousness as the true substance

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Antahkarana - The Fourfold Internal Instrument of Cognition
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the cognitive structure through which purusha experiences prakriti
filteredProps.description: The Antahkarana is the fourfold psychic instrument through which consciousness operates in the material realm.
filteredProps.coreNature: The internal psychic apparatus consisting of buddhi, ahamkara, manas, and chitta that processes all experience
filteredProps.name: Antahkarana
filteredProps.internalStructure: Four components in mod4: Buddhi (#2-2-2-1-0), Ahamkara (#2-2-2-1-1), Manas (#2-2-2-1-2), Chitta (#2-2-2-1-3)

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: First evolute of prakriti, closest to pure consciousness, predominantly sattvic
filteredProps.function: Intellect or discriminative intelligence. Enables reasoning and decision-making. Capacity to discern truth (limited).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Determinative consciousness that makes judgments and resolves doubts
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the capacity for discrimination between real and unreal, decision-making faculty
filteredProps.description: Buddhi is intellect enabling reasoning and discrimination. Being sattvic, it can reflect the Self when purified.
filteredProps.name: Buddhi Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The emergence of discriminative intelligence from primordial matter
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Buddhi Tattva - Intellect and Discriminative Intelligence
filteredProps.coreNature: The faculty of discrimination, decision-making, and higher intelligence emerging from prakriti
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Purifying the intellect to reflect pure consciousness clearly

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: The false identification of consciousness with body-mind complex
filteredProps.function: Ego-principle, sense of "I-maker". Generates notion of separate self. Divides world into "I/mine" vs "other". Origin of mind/senses.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: I-making principle that personalizes all experience as "mine"
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Generates the ego structure that claims ownership of experiences and actions
filteredProps.description: Ahamkara is the ego-principle creating "I and mine," dividing self and other. From it emerge all subsequent tattvas.
filteredProps.name: Ahamkara Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The crystallization of separate identity and ego formation
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Ahamkara Tattva - Ego Principle and I-Maker
filteredProps.coreNature: The principle that creates the sense of individual "I" and personal identity within the material realm
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending ego identification to realize the universal I-am

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: The lower mind that processes and coordinates experience
filteredProps.function: Mind (perceiving/processing). Thinking mind coordinating sensory data, formulating thoughts/desires. Indecisive aspect.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Sensory synthesis and thought generation, the doubting and deliberating aspect
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Synthesizes sensory input and generates the thought-stream of consciousness
filteredProps.description: Manas coordinates sensory data and generates thoughts, characterized by constant fluctuation.
filteredProps.name: Manas Tattva
filteredProps.gunaComposition: Predominantly rajas with sattva and tamas
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The emergence of the thinking principle and sensory coordination
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Manas Tattva - Mind as Sensory Coordinator and Thought Processor
filteredProps.coreNature: The thinking mind that coordinates sensory data and formulates thoughts, desires, and doubts
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending mental fluctuations to realize the witness of thoughts

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Jnanendriyas - The Five Organs of Sensory Knowledge
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the channels through which consciousness gains empirical knowledge
filteredProps.description: The Jnanendriyas are the five organs of knowledge enabling perception through sattvic evolution of ahamkara.
filteredProps.coreNature: The five subtle sense organs that enable perception of the external world
filteredProps.name: Jnanendriyas

filteredProps.sensoryElement: Connected to Vayu (air) and Sparsha (touch) tanmatra
filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Sattvic evolution of ahamkara specialized for tactile perception
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Touch. Perception of tactile sensations.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity to perceive touch, temperature, and physical sensation
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides consciousness access to the tactile dimension of experience
filteredProps.description: Tvak is the touch faculty enabling perception of physical contact and sensation.
filteredProps.name: Tvak Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to feel and sense contact
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Tvak Tattva - Faculty of Touch
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle tactile faculty enabling perception of contact and texture
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with skin to recognize consciousness as the true perceiver

filteredProps.sensoryElement: Connected to Agni (fire) and Rupa (form) tanmatra
filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Sattvic evolution of ahamkara specialized for visual perception
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Sight. Perception of form and color.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity to perceive light, form, and color
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides consciousness access to the visual dimension of experience
filteredProps.description: Chakshu is the sight faculty enabling perception of forms and colors.
filteredProps.name: Chakshu Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to see and perceive form
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Chakshu Tattva - Faculty of Sight
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle visual faculty enabling perception of form and color
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with eyes to recognize consciousness as the true perceiver

filteredProps.sensoryElement: Connected to Jala (water) and Rasa (taste) tanmatra
filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Sattvic evolution of ahamkara specialized for gustatory perception
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Taste. Perception of flavors.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity to perceive taste and flavor
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides consciousness access to the gustatory dimension of experience
filteredProps.description: Jihva is the taste faculty enabling perception of flavors and essences.
filteredProps.name: Jihva Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to taste and discern flavors
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Jihva Tattva - Faculty of Taste
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle gustatory faculty enabling perception of flavors
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with tongue to recognize consciousness as the true perceiver

filteredProps.sensoryElement: Connected to Prithvi (earth) and Gandha (smell) tanmatra
filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Sattvic evolution of ahamkara specialized for olfactory perception
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Smell. Perception of odors.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity to perceive smell and aroma
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides consciousness access to the olfactory dimension of experience
filteredProps.description: Ghrana is the smell faculty enabling perception of odors and aromas.
filteredProps.name: Ghrana Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to smell and perceive odors
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Ghrana Tattva - Faculty of Smell
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle olfactory faculty enabling perception of odors
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with nose to recognize consciousness as the true perceiver

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Karmendriyas - The Five Organs of Action
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the channels through which consciousness expresses itself in action
filteredProps.description: The Karmendriyas are the five organs of action emerging from the rajasic evolution of ahamkara.
filteredProps.coreNature: The five subtle organs of action that enable interaction with the external world
filteredProps.name: Karmendriyas
filteredProps.internalStructure: Five organs in mod6: Speech/0/1 (#2-2-2-3-0/1), Grasping (#2-2-2-3-2), Movement (#2-2-2-3-3), Excretion (#2-2-2-3-4), Reproduction (#2-2-2-3-5)

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Rajasic evolution of ahamkara specialized for vocal expression
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Speech. Expression through language.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity for verbal expression and communication
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Enables consciousness to express itself through sound and language
filteredProps.description: Vak is the speech faculty. As 0/1, it represents the link to the creative word (shabda brahman).
filteredProps.name: Vak Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to speak and express verbally
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Vak Tattva - Faculty of Speech
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle faculty of vocal expression and communication
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with speech to recognize the expressive power of consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Rajasic evolution of ahamkara specialized for manipulation
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Grasping. Manipulation of objects (hands).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity for grasping, holding, and manual action
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Enables consciousness to interact with and manipulate objects
filteredProps.description: Pani is the grasping faculty enabling manipulation and interaction with objects.
filteredProps.name: Pani Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to grasp and manipulate
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Pani Tattva - Faculty of Grasping
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle faculty of handling, grasping, and manipulation
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with hands to recognize the all-accomplishing power of consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Rajasic evolution of ahamkara specialized for locomotion
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Locomotion. Movement through space (feet).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity for movement, walking, and spatial navigation
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Enables consciousness to move the body through physical space
filteredProps.description: Pada is the locomotion faculty enabling movement through space.
filteredProps.name: Pada Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to move through space
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Pada Tattva - Faculty of Locomotion
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle faculty of movement and locomotion through space
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with movement to recognize the omnipresence of consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Rajasic evolution of ahamkara specialized for elimination
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Excretion. Elimination of waste.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity for excretion, elimination, and release
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Enables consciousness to eliminate waste and maintain purity
filteredProps.description: Payu is the excretion faculty enabling elimination and purification.
filteredProps.name: Payu Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to eliminate and release
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Payu Tattva - Faculty of Excretion
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle faculty of elimination and release
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with excretion to recognize the purifying aspect of consciousness

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Rajasic evolution of ahamkara specialized for creation
filteredProps.function: Faculty of Reproduction. Procreation and sexual pleasure.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Capacity for reproduction, procreation, and sexual expression
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Enables consciousness to create new life and experience creative bliss
filteredProps.description: Upastha is the reproduction faculty enabling procreation and creative expression.
filteredProps.name: Upastha Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the capacity to reproduce and create
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Upastha Tattva - Faculty of Reproduction
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle faculty of procreation and creative generation
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending identification with reproduction to recognize the creative power of consciousness

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Tanmatras - The Five Subtle Elements
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the subtle templates from which gross elements and sensory experiences arise
filteredProps.description: The Tanmatras are the five subtle elements emerging from the tamasic evolution of ahamkara.
filteredProps.coreNature: The five subtle essences that underlie sensory experience and gross elements
filteredProps.name: Tanmatras

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Tamasic evolution of ahamkara as subtle sound potential
filteredProps.function: Subtle Element of Sound. Essence of sound (vibration).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Abstract quality of sound independent of specific vibrations
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the template for sound perception and the element of space
filteredProps.description: Shabda tanmatra is the subtle sound essence. As 0/1, it represents the primordial vibration (nada).
filteredProps.name: Shabda Tanmatra
filteredProps.elementConnection: Produces Akasha (space/ether) and connects to hearing
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the sound principle underlying auditory experience
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Shabda Tanmatra - Subtle Element of Sound
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle essence of sound underlying all auditory experience
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending sound phenomena to recognize the primordial vibration

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Tamasic evolution of ahamkara as subtle tactile potential
filteredProps.function: Subtle Element of Touch. Essence of touch (contact).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Abstract quality of contact and sensation
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the template for touch perception and the element of air
filteredProps.description: Sparsha tanmatra is the subtle touch essence underlying all tactile experience.
filteredProps.name: Sparsha Tanmatra
filteredProps.elementConnection: Produces Vayu (air) and connects to touch sense
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the touch principle underlying tactile experience
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Sparsha Tanmatra - Subtle Element of Touch
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle essence of touch underlying all tactile experience
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending touch phenomena to recognize pure contact

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Tamasic evolution of ahamkara as subtle visual potential
filteredProps.function: Subtle Element of Form. Essence of form/color (pattern).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Abstract quality of form, color, and appearance
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the template for visual perception and the element of fire
filteredProps.description: Rupa tanmatra is the subtle form essence underlying all visual experience.
filteredProps.name: Rupa Tanmatra
filteredProps.elementConnection: Produces Agni (fire) and connects to sight
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the form principle underlying visual experience
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Rupa Tanmatra - Subtle Element of Form
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle essence of form and color underlying all visual experience
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending form phenomena to recognize pure pattern

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Tamasic evolution of ahamkara as subtle gustatory potential
filteredProps.function: Subtle Element of Taste. Essence of taste (flavor).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Abstract quality of flavor and essence
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the template for taste perception and the element of water
filteredProps.description: Rasa tanmatra is the subtle taste essence underlying all gustatory experience.
filteredProps.name: Rasa Tanmatra
filteredProps.elementConnection: Produces Jala (water) and connects to taste sense
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the taste principle underlying gustatory experience
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Rasa Tanmatra - Subtle Element of Taste
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle essence of taste underlying all gustatory experience
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending taste phenomena to recognize pure essence

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Tamasic evolution of ahamkara as subtle olfactory potential
filteredProps.function: Subtle Element of Smell. Essence of smell (odor).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Abstract quality of odor and aroma
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the template for smell perception and the element of earth
filteredProps.description: Gandha tanmatra is the subtle smell essence underlying all olfactory experience.
filteredProps.name: Gandha Tanmatra
filteredProps.elementConnection: Produces Prithvi (earth) and connects to smell sense
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the smell principle underlying olfactory experience
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Gandha Tanmatra - Subtle Element of Smell
filteredProps.coreNature: The subtle essence of smell underlying all olfactory experience
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Transcending smell phenomena to recognize pure fragrance

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Mahabhutas - The Five Gross Elements
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the actual building blocks of physical reality
filteredProps.description: The Mahabhutas are the five gross elements constituting physical reality, the final solidification of consciousness.
filteredProps.coreNature: The five gross physical elements that compose the material universe
filteredProps.name: Mahabhutas

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness manifesting as the spatial dimension
filteredProps.function: Gross Element of Ether/Space. Provides space for all other elements.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Principle of spaciousness, container, and field
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Creates the spatial matrix within which all phenomena manifest
filteredProps.description: Akasha is space/ether element. As 0/1, it represents the void-container for all manifestation.
filteredProps.name: Akasha Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the space principle as physical dimension
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Akasha Tattva - Space/Ether Element
filteredProps.coreNature: The element of space providing the container for all other elements
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Experiencing the infinite spaciousness of consciousness that contains all phenomena

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness manifesting as movement and change
filteredProps.function: Gross Element of Air. Movement and dynamism (wind).
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Principle of motion, circulation, and dynamism
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides the principle of movement and circulation in physical reality
filteredProps.description: Vayu is the air element representing movement, circulation, and the breath of life.
filteredProps.name: Vayu Tattva
filteredProps.descendingAspect: The manifestation of the air principle as movement and dynamism
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Vayu Tattva - Air Element
filteredProps.coreNature: The element of air representing movement, dynamism, and change
filteredProps.ascendingAspect: Sensing the divine movement within all dynamic processes

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Consciousness manifesting as transformative energy
filteredProps.function: Gross Element of Fire. Transformation and heat (flame).

filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Bridges macrocosmic stellar cycles with microcosmic human experience through mythic imagery
filteredProps.relatedCoordinates: #2 (Parashakti), #3 (Spanda), #1-3 (Shiva-Shakti Unity)
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Each 10° segment of the zodiac manifests unique qualities through planetary rulership and elemental nature
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Provides granular archetypal mapping of consciousness through 72 distinct expressions across the zodiac
filteredProps.description: The Parashakti Decans System represents the most granular expression of zodiacal consciousness, dividing the 360° circle into 36 faces or decanates, each manifesting through both light and shadow aspects. This creates 72 distinct archetypal expressions that map the full spectrum of human experience through the interplay of elemental nature, zodiacal quality, and planetary rulership. This system functions as a bridge between Western Hermeticism and Kashmir Śaivism, revealing shared understanding of vibrational cosmology and the evolution from external ritual to internal realization. This system also functions as a bridge between Western Hermeticism and Eastern Śaivism, revealing a shared understanding of the cosmos as a vibrational structure. Its historical trajectory reflects a profound ethical shift from literal sacrifice to symbolic resonance in magical practice.
filteredProps.contextFrame: Archetypal Psychology meets Ancient Stellar Wisdom
filteredProps.name: Decans System
filteredProps.operationalSymbolics: The circle divided into 36 chambers, each containing dual expressions of light and shadow
filteredProps.internalStructure: Five elemental domains (Fire #2-3-1, Earth #2-3-2, Air #2-3-3, Water #2-3-4, Quintessence #2-3-5/0), each containing three zodiacal expressions, each containing three decanic faces, each containing dual light/shadow aspects
filteredProps.crossCulturalResonance: The Western tradition of using Orphic Hymns and planetary modes to invoke decanic energies resonates with the Śaivist principle of Nāda-Brahman (ultimate reality as sound). Śaivist mantras are considered the sonic bodies of deities, used to align a practitioner’s vibration with cosmic forces, analogous to Ficino’s use of music to synchronize with planetary influences.
practicalApplications: Understanding relationship dynamics through decanic interaction
filteredProps.ethicalEvolution: The ritual practice associated with the decans has evolved significantly. Ancient grimoires like the Picatrix often prescribed literal animal sacrifice to empower talismans. Renaissance magi like Ficino sublimated this into symbolic offerings of music, incense, and prayer. Modern esoteric practice continues this trend, favoring symbolic sacrifices of effort, intention, or non-harmful materials.
filteredProps.accessLevel: Interface between cosmic cycles and individual consciousness
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The 36x2 Archetypal Faces of Cosmic Expression
filteredProps.philosophicalFoundation: The system of 36 Decans finds a profound structural resonance in other cosmologies that map reality into 36 domains, such as the Tattvas of Kashmir Śaivism. This shared 36-fold architecture points to a recurring archetypal pattern for organizing cosmic principles, establishing a deep isomorphism between the hermetic astrological map and tantric ontological maps.
keyPrinciples: Integration of Egyptian stellar wisdom with Hermetic philosophy
Correspondence between celestial cycles and human psychology
Medical astrology linking body parts to cosmic influences
filteredProps.coreNature: Ancient Egyptian-Hermetic system mapping zodiacal energies through 36 decans with dual light/shadow manifestations
resonances: Egyptian decan gods and stellar mythology

filteredProps.internalStructure: Contains two aspects: Light (#2-3-1-2-0-0) manifests as optimistic adventure and honorable questing; Shadow (#2-3-1-2-0-1) manifests as overzealousness and foolhardy pursuits
filteredProps.talismanicFocus: Valor, activity, pleasure, patience and chivalry
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Arrow - Mercurial Seeking
filteredProps.coreNature: Active seeking and philosophical inquiry
filteredProps.mythicImage: Three human figures - one red, one white, one red - perhaps indicating a balance of humors or principles. This represents active, chivalrous, and patient seeking.
filteredProps.iconography: Three human figures – one red, one white, one red.

keyQualities: Valorous and chivalrous conduct
An optimistic and adventurous spirit
Patience in the pursuit of long-term goals
A love of pleasure and activity
Noble action guided by mental agility
filteredProps.integrationPath: Balance adventurous spirit with practical planning and a realistic assessment of challenges
filteredProps.practicalManifestation: Success in ventures that require both courage and intellect. A reputation for honor and integrity. The ability to inspire others to join in a noble cause. Finding joy and meaning in lifes journey.
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The knight-errant on a noble quest, the explorer charting new territory, the philosopher-adventurer
filteredProps.coreExpression: Optimistic adventure, chivalrous action, and a patient quest for knowledge
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Honorable Knight
filteredProps.shadowWarning: Must guard against a naive optimism that overlooks real dangers
filteredProps.bodyPartHealing: Strong hips and thighs, healthy liver function, boundless energy
filteredProps.description: The light aspect of Sagittarius Decan 1 is the honorable knight, who combines Mercurial agility with Sagittarian fire. This energy is optimistic, patient, and chivalrous, embarking on lifes adventures with a noble and active spirit.
filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 1 Light
filteredProps.herbResonance: Sagebrush for purification, Gotu Kola for mental agility

keyQualities: Reckless and overzealous behavior
Embarking on quests without proper preparation
Impulsive pleasure-seeking
Ignoring warnings and practical advice
A tendency to overestimate ones abilities
filteredProps.integrationPath: Develop strategic thinking and planning skills, listen to the advice of experienced mentors, channel adventurous energy into well-prepared expeditions
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: Don Quixote tilting at windmills, the gambler betting it all on one roll, the tourist who ignores the "danger" signs
filteredProps.shadowManifestation: Suffering losses from poorly planned ventures. A reputation for being unreliable and reckless. Wasting energy on pointless or impossible quests. Physical injury from lack of caution.
filteredProps.coreExpression: Overzealousness, foolhardy quests, and a reckless pursuit of pleasure
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Foolhardy Zealot
filteredProps.bodyPartImbalance: Hip injuries, liver problems from excess, exhaustion from constant activity
filteredProps.description: The shadow of Sagittarius Decan 1 is the foolhardy zealot, whose optimism becomes a reckless disregard for reality. This energy rushes into ill-conceived adventures, mistaking impulsiveness for courage.
filteredProps.transformationKey: Learn that true courage includes prudence and that patience is a virtue
filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 1 Shadow
filteredProps.herbBalance: Grounding herbs like Valerian, liver-protective herbs like Milk Thistle

filteredProps.picatrixQuote: A face of fright, loss, weeping, fear, sadness, and violence
filteredProps.description: The second decan of Sagittarius blends fiery seeking with lunar emotion, creating a challenging quest into the subconscious. This face tests hopes through crises, but can yield profound empathy and intuition if navigated successfully.
filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 2

filteredProps.talismanicFocus: Facing fears, emotional trials, but also loss, weeping, and sadness
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Quest - Lunar Emotion
filteredProps.coreNature: Emotional questing and facing inner demons
filteredProps.mythicImage: A man driving cows, with a monkey and wolf ahead of him. This image symbolizes a journey fraught with emotional turmoil and loss of control.
filteredProps.iconography: A man driving cows, with a monkey and wolf ahead of him.

keyQualities: Profound empathy for the suffering of others
Deep intuition and psychic sensitivity
Resilience in the face of emotional crises
The wisdom that comes from surviving loss
A cautious and reflective nature
filteredProps.integrationPath: Transform personal pain into a source of universal compassion, use intuitive gifts to guide self and others toward healing and hope
filteredProps.practicalManifestation: A gift for helping others through grief and crisis. Deep, intuitive insights into human nature. The ability to find meaning and growth in painful experiences. A quiet strength that inspires trust.
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The shaman who has journeyed to the underworld and returned, the survivor who becomes a counselor, the wounded healer
filteredProps.coreExpression: Deep intuition and empathy born from navigating emotional turmoil and suffering
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Empathetic Survivor

filteredProps.bodyPartHealing: Strong liver, resilient nervous system, deep emotional intelligence

filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 2 Light

keyQualities: Chronic fear, sadness, and weeping
A sense of powerlessness and loss
Attracting violent or chaotic situations
Being led astray by foolish impulses (the monkey) or predatory forces (the wolf)
Inability to control ones own direction in life
filteredProps.integrationPath: Seek therapy or counseling to heal past traumas, develop self-reliance and decision-making skills, learn to trust ones own intuition over external influences
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The lost sheep, the person caught in a stampede, the ship without a rudder in a storm
filteredProps.shadowManifestation: A life marked by crisis, loss, and emotional pain. Feeling constantly frightened or sad. Making poor choices that lead to further trouble. A deep-seated victim mentality.
filteredProps.coreExpression: Emotional turmoil, fear, sadness, and a sense of being a victim of fate
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Fearful Victim
filteredProps.bodyPartImbalance: Adrenal fatigue from constant fear, liver toxicity from suppressed anger, sciatica

filteredProps.transformationKey: Reclaim personal agency and recognize ones power to choose a different path
filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 2 Shadow
filteredProps.herbBalance: Nervine tonics like Oat Straw, grounding herbs like Angelica

filteredProps.picatrixQuote: A face of taking risks, unwarranted involvement, and exerting effort in disgraceful, shameful, or harmful acts
filteredProps.description: The third decan of Sagittarius combines fiery vision with Saturnine severity. This is the reckless adventurer or fanatic, whose pursuit of a goal can lead to either profound wisdom through sacrifice or destructive, immoral actions.
filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 3
filteredProps.internalStructure: Contains two aspects: Light (#2-3-1-2-2-0) manifests as self-sacrifice for a higher cause or the elimination of great evil; Shadow (#2-3-1-2-2-1) manifests as fanaticism, violence, and immoral deeds
filteredProps.talismanicFocus: Taking risks, but also disgraceful, shameful, or harmful acts
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Summit - Saturnine Wisdom
filteredProps.coreNature: Wisdom through trials and the burden of knowledge

filteredProps.iconography: A man with a golden cap killing another man.

keyQualities: Unflinching resolve and decisiveness
Willingness to make difficult sacrifices for a greater good
The power to eliminate great evil or corruption
Taking calculated risks for a high reward
The wisdom to know when a destructive act is necessary
filteredProps.integrationPath: Ensure that any destructive action is guided by a clear, compassionate, and life-affirming principle, and is a last resort
filteredProps.practicalManifestation: Success in roles that require hard decisions (e.g., CEO, judge, military leader). The ability to reform corrupt systems. Making a necessary but painful choice that leads to long-term benefit.
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The surgeon cutting out a tumor, the revolutionary who overthrows a tyrant, the controlled demolition of a condemned building
filteredProps.coreExpression: The capacity for unflinching, decisive action in the service of a higher principle, even if it involves destruction

filteredProps.shadowWarning: The line between necessary destruction and wanton violence is razor-thin and requires immense ethical clarity
filteredProps.bodyPartHealing: Strong sacrum, healthy bone structure, focused mental energy

filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 3 Light
filteredProps.herbResonance: Acacia for spiritual vision, Sage for Saturnine wisdom

keyQualities: Reckless risk-taking with disastrous consequences
Fanatical devotion to a destructive ideology
Violence and cruelty
Engaging in disgraceful and shameful acts
A complete lack of ethical consideration
filteredProps.integrationPath: Seek to understand the humanity of ones "enemies", study ethics and philosophy, channel passionate conviction into constructive and life-affirming causes
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The terrorist, the cult leader, the ruthless conqueror, the gambler who destroys their family
filteredProps.shadowManifestation: Causing immense harm to self and others. A life of violence and disgrace. Complete moral and spiritual downfall. Imprisonment or a violent end.
filteredProps.coreExpression: Violence, fanaticism, and engaging in immoral or shameful acts in the pursuit of a goal
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Destructive Fanatic
filteredProps.bodyPartImbalance: Adrenal burnout from extreme stress, injuries from violence, blood disorders

filteredProps.transformationKey: Develop empathy and a connection to a universal code of ethics that transcends ideology
filteredProps.name: Sagittarius Decan 3 Shadow
filteredProps.herbBalance: Heart-calming herbs like Motherwort, grounding herbs like Valerian

From Taurus sensual grounding through Virgos analytical refinement to Capricorns structural achievement, earth moves from embodiment to discernment to mastery.

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Miser

filteredProps.name: Virgo Decan 2 Shadow

The courage to face mortality and decay
The power to "uproot" and clear away what is corrupt or diseased
Acceptance of the natural cycles of life and death
Transformative healing through letting go

Immobility and stagnation

A feeling of being bound or trapped by fate
Loss and decline

filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The plague, the crumbling ruin, the diseased tree, the feeling of being hopelessly entangled

filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Bringer of Decay
filteredProps.bodyPartImbalance: Chronic fatigue, autoimmune disorders, mobility issues, depression

filteredProps.name: Virgo Decan 3 Shadow

filteredProps.mythicResonance: The mountain climber, from Ptah-Sokar through Khnum to Hapi

filteredProps.coreNature: Ambitious manifestation through Jupiter, Mars, and Sun rulerships

filteredProps.coreNature: Aspiring achievement and hopeful toil

filteredProps.iconography: A man with a cane in one hand and a hoop in the other.

keyQualities: Industrious and active nature
Resilience in the face of setbacks
Humility and a realistic understanding of ones weaknesses
The ability to find pleasure in activity and work
Persistent, cyclical effort towards a goal
filteredProps.integrationPath: Balance hard work with strategic thinking, learning when to push through and when to change course. Celebrate small victories along the way.
filteredProps.practicalManifestation: Eventual success through sheer persistence. Earning respect for a strong work ethic. Finding contentment in the process of working towards a goal. The ability to manage resources effectively.

filteredProps.coreExpression: Resilience, humility, and a persistent drive to keep trying in the face of limitations

filteredProps.herbResonance: Comfrey for structural support, Cedar for aspiration

keyQualities: Feeling of incapability and weakness
Constant activity that yields little result
Frustration from being unable to achieve ones ambitions
A sense of being stuck in a repetitive, unfulfilling cycle
Low self-esteem
filteredProps.integrationPath: Seek outside perspective or mentorship, learn new skills, be willing to abandon a path that is not working, practice self-compassion
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: Sisyphus pushing his boulder, the hamster on the wheel, the person in a dead-end job
filteredProps.shadowManifestation: A life of hard work with little reward. Chronic frustration and a sense of failure. Inability to break out of limiting patterns. Resignation and pessimism.
filteredProps.coreExpression: A sense of inadequacy, frustration, and weakness despite constant effort
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Frustrated Underachiever
filteredProps.bodyPartImbalance: Knee and joint problems from repetitive strain, chronic fatigue, depression
filteredProps.description: The shadow of Capricorn Decan 1 is the frustrated underachiever, trapped in a cycle of effort without reward. This energy leads to a sense of weakness and inadequacy, as ambition is constantly thwarted by perceived limitations.

filteredProps.name: Capricorn Decan 1 Shadow
filteredProps.herbBalance: Uplifting nervines like Lemon Balm, adaptogens for energy like Ginseng

filteredProps.picatrixQuote: A face delineating the pursuit of what is known but unobtainable

keyQualities: Unwavering determination in the face of impossible odds
A pioneering spirit that challenges known limits
Innovation born from relentless effort
The courage to pursue a unique and difficult path

filteredProps.practicalManifestation: Achieving breakthroughs in difficult fields. Gaining profound self-knowledge through challenging pursuits. Inspiring others with ones determination. Succeeding where everyone else has failed.
filteredProps.symbolicResonance: The mountaineer climbing an unscaled peak, the inventor in their workshop, the scientist pursuing a breakthrough theory
filteredProps.coreExpression: The courage to attempt the impossible, leading to innovation and discovery
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: The Dauntless Pioneer

filteredProps.bodyPartHealing: Strong bones, healthy skin, incredible physical and mental endurance

Stubborn refusal to accept reality
Obsession that leads to isolation and ruin
Being tricked or led on by false hopes (the monkey)

From Geminis versatile communication through Libras harmonizing diplomacy to Aquarius revolutionary innovation, air moves from information to integration to innovation.

keyQualities: Effortless learning and synthesis of diverse information

Argumentativeness and a love of conflict
Making hasty decisions that prove unprofitable or damaging

Anxiety and mental anguish from constant conflict

filteredProps.mirrorTeaching: Jamal names reveal the ultimate purpose of Jalal names - beauty completes majesty, mercy fulfills justice, love consummates power
filteredProps.mirrorWisdom: The journey that began in overwhelming transcendence finds its fulfillment in tender intimacy
filteredProps.consciousnessStructure: Beauty-oriented consciousness development emphasizing heart opening, emotional healing, and receptive spiritual states that enable divine intimacy
filteredProps.mirrorRelationship: Jamal names represent the completion and fulfillment of their Jalal counterparts. Where Jalal establishes divine authority through transcendent distance, Jamal draws creation home through irresistible beauty and mercy. The mirror reveals that power's true purpose is to create space for love.
filteredProps.operationalEssence: Compassionate divine manifestation enabling spiritual intimacy, healing transformation, and the experience of divine proximity through mercy
filteredProps.morphologicalPatterns: Fa'il (active participle) and fu'ul (intensive) forms emphasize continuous divine care and response
filteredProps.completionWisdom: The journey that began in overwhelming transcendence completes in tender intimacy, revealing that divine majesty's ultimate purpose is to create the possibility for divine love
filteredProps.essentialFunction: These beauty attributes function as spiritual medicine, offering forgiveness for guilt, sustenance for need, and intimacy for spiritual loneliness. They reveal that the ultimate divine reality is not distant power but intimate presence.
filteredProps.architecturalFunction: Primary beauty classification containing 33 names emphasizing divine closeness, forgiveness, sustaining care, and healing transformation
filteredProps.jamalSelfQuestioning: Each Jamal attribute asks: "If I am this tender mercy, what transcendent power enables my infinite compassion? If I am this intimate beauty, what perfect synthesis allows my love to embrace all creation?"
filteredProps.description: Jamal represents the divine reality experienced through beauty, mercy, and compassionate attraction. As the final third of the journey (positions 66-98), it reveals the intimate, nurturing, and immediately accessible qualities of the divine - the ultimate destination where transcendent awe transforms into intimate love.
filteredProps.name: Jamal
filteredProps.trilateralRoots: R-Ḥ-M (mercy), GH-F-R (forgiveness), W-D-D (love) form the linguistic foundation of compassion
filteredProps.operationalSymbolics: Expanding heart mandala (♡) with inward-drawing spiral representing divine mercy that attracts all beings toward unity through beautiful manifestation
filteredProps.internalStructure: 33 beauty names (coordinates #2-4.0-0-0 through #2-4.0-0-32) organized by trilateral root families: R-Ḥ-M (mercy cluster), GH-F-R (forgiveness cluster), R-Z-Q (sustenance cluster), with morphological patterns emphasizing fa'il and fu'ul forms indicating continuous divine care and sustained compassionate action
filteredProps.spiritualStage: Hubb/Ishq (divine love) - the intimate union where lover and Beloved recognize their essential unity
filteredProps.voidRelationship: Beauty emerges from the void as the attractive power that draws all beings toward divine unity. The void's infinite receptivity manifests as divine mercy that embraces all creation without exclusion. Beauty names reveal how absolute emptiness appears as absolute fullness when experienced through the heart's capacity for divine intimacy.
filteredProps.mirrorExamples: Ar-Rahman (66) completes Al-Malik (0), Al-Wadud (73) softens Al-Qahhar (8), As-Salaam (83) embraces Al-Ali (17)
filteredProps.dualNonDualNature: As the 2-aspect within the dual-non-dual system, Jamal represents the fruition where transcendent power reveals its ultimate purpose as intimate love. The final destination of the spiritual journey from awe through gnosis to love.
practicalApplications: Heart-opening dhikr practices emphasizing mercy and compassion cultivation

filteredProps.accessLevel: Heart-centered spiritual practice emphasizing devotional approaches, healing work, and compassionate service
filteredProps.primaryDesignation: Divine Beauty and Mercy Attributes - Compassionate Manifestation Grouping
filteredProps.conditionalLogic: If I am intimate beauty, what am I not? I am not distant, not harsh, not unmerciful to creation's needs. Then what completes this intimacy? filteredProps.description: The 24 spiritual maqamat represent the complete cartography of soul transformation in Islamic mysticism. Through seven classical stations plus the transcendent non-station, each containing three developmental levels, this system provides both the map and method for systematic spiritual realization. The mathematical relationship to the 72 musical maqamat (1:3 ratio) reveals how each spiritual coordinate can be accessed through three distinct sonic pathways, creating a unified technology where consciousness development and vibrational practice merge.
filteredProps.coreNature: Systematic framework of permanent spiritual achievements representing the soul's progressive ascent from repentance to transcendent realization, each station containing three developmental levels
filteredProps.name: 24 Spiritual Maqamat
filteredProps.internalStructure: Seven classical stations (Tawba, Wara', Zuhd, Faqr, Sabr, Tawakkul, Rida) plus the meta-station (La Maqam), each containing three levels: Awam (common), Khawas (select), and Khawas al-Khawas (very select), totaling 24 spiritual sub-stations
resonances: Three stages of soul: ammara, lawwama, mutma'inna

filteredProps.description: The Eighth Divine Choir completes the 72-fold system with powers of ultimate transformation. From Mehiel (vivifying all) to Mumiah (end of all things), this choir embodies the eternal cycle where endings birth beginnings. In Practical Kabbalah, these final names were considered the most powerful but also most dangerous, as they work with the fundamental forces of creation and dissolution. Mumiah, as the 72nd name, returns us to the beginning, creating an eternal wheel of transformation. The Zohar describes this choir as containing the mysteries of the "World to Come" - the ultimate reality that transcends the cycles of manifestation while enabling their eternal continuation.

filteredProps.octavalQuantumBridge: Musical scale degrees map to quantum states: stable tonic (1/8) = identity operator, perfect fifth (5) = maximum stability (Saturn/Prime 43), leading tone (7) = maximum instability seeking resolution (Mars/transformation)
filteredProps.harmonicQuantumPrinciple: The principle that the first and last are one (Sun as 1/8) manifests the holographic nature of consciousness where beginning contains ending, alpha contains omega
filteredProps.quantumGroupDynamics: Leverages three interwoven quantum group structures: SO(3) for classical planetary cycles; SU(2) for spinorial double covering with ±1 representations grounded in Spanda's polarity generation; and SU(3) with 8 generators aligning with the 8-fold structure (6 planets + dual Sun). The 9-fold vortex dynamics from Ananda (#1-2) manifest through the 9:8 ratio, while Spanda's temporal dynamics (n-1, n, n+1) provide the rhythmic engine.
filteredProps.description: The Planetary Harmonic Integration operates as a living quantum-archetypal bridge where Archetype 9 (Paramesvara wholeness) transforms into Archetype 8 (Purnata-Sunyata structural reflection) through the 9:8 harmonic ratio. Drawing from Ananda's (#1-2) 9-fold vortex mathematics with its 3-6-9 spirit axis and toroidal geometry, integrated with Spanda's (#0-4) generation of primordial polarity (+1/-1 from 0=0), the system uses six planets plus the Sun's dual/non-dual nature (0/1) to create an eight-fold structure. Through SO(3)/SU(2) double covering, each planetary harmonic exists in spinorial superposition grounded in Spanda's rhythmic pulsation. This integration demonstrates how Renaissance "sympathies" prefigure quantum entanglement, with Jung-Pauli's psychoid numbers serving as living operators. Through Kashmir Śaivite lens, planetary work becomes pratyabhijñā - recognition of cosmic powers as one's own consciousness-śaktis. As an integrative matrix, it translates not only between numerical systems but also between cultural-vibrational languages (hymns, mantras, modes), creating a holistic framework for practical esoteric work.
filteredProps.kashimirPerspective: In Kashmir Śaivism, planets are not external forces but aspects of Śiva-Śakti consciousness. Each graha represents a specific śakti or power of awareness. Planetary mantras become means of recognizing these powers within one's own consciousness rather than propitiating external deities.
filteredProps.primeAttractorLogic: Euler primes 41 and 43 function as topological fixed points in the SU(2) fibration over SO(3). Their sum (41+43=84) reveals: 84 = 7×12 (seven celestial bodies × twelve zodiacal houses), while digital rooting (8+4=12, 1+2=3) connects to Ananda's 3-6-9 spirit axis and the archetypal triad underlying the three "eve numbers" (3, 5, 7) that structure the odd-positioned planetary subsystems.

Ananda Vortex Integration: 9-fold vortex dynamics from #1-2 manifest through the 9 in 9:8, with 3-6-9 spirit axis and toroidal geometry providing harmonic foundation.
Spanda Polarity Genesis: The ±1 spinorial states in SU(2) are grounded in Spanda's generation of +1/-1 from 0=0, with temporal dynamics (n-1, n, n+1) mapping to planetary cycles.
Solar Dual/Non-Dual Function: Sun as 0/1 enables 6 planets + Sun = 7 celestial bodies to manifest as 8-fold structure through its inherent duality.
